prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.41) and extreme seasonality variance (0.867), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA best tracks trend while ARIMA captures seasonality, with FT_svr dominating overall RMSE. These divergent strengths across trend/seasonality dimensions strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary model behaviors.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "CWT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 2.4117,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.41) and extreme seasonality variance (0.867), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA best tracks trend while ARIMA captures seasonality, with FT_svr dominating overall RMSE. These divergent strengths across trend/seasonality dimensions strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "direction_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates per-horizon selection is valuable. relative_spread_mean=4.46 shows extreme model disagreement, supporting DBA (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). RMSE gap between FT_svr(41.03) and NaiveSeasonal(129.97) is 3.17x (>30%), favoring top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.867. direction_focus preset chosen due to avg POCID=38.1 < 45 (low directional accuracy)."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No ≥4 of same type exists. All candidates are safe from leakage (baselines never leak; selection/weighted use anti-leakage rolling/expanding per eval_config). Universe leaderboards show no candidate that dominates all current types. Score margin top2=0.014 indicates balanced set. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["trimmed_mean_r0.25", "robust_median"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=4.46 ≥ 0.25) favors trimmed_mean and robust methods. Stability issue (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.68, unstable=true) requires robust_median. n_windows=3 is low, so stronger regularization needed - adding trimmed_mean_r0.25 provides better RMSE (66.29 vs current ~70). Universe shows trimmed_mean_r0.30 has best RMSE at 66.17. Current set already has dba_combination and topk selection methods, no need to add more weighted candidates.", "changes": ["added trimmed_mean_r0.25 for high disagreement", "added robust_median for stability issues"], "when_good": "When relative_spread_mean < 0.25 and rmse_std_over_rmse < 0.3"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.41) and extreme seasonality variance (0.867), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA best tracks trend while ARIMA captures seasonality, with FT_svr dominating overall RMSE. These divergent strengths across trend/seasonality dimensions strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary model behaviors.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates per-horizon selection is valuable. relative_spread_mean=4.46 shows extreme model disagreement, supporting DBA (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). RMSE gap between FT_svr(41.03) and NaiveSeasonal(129.97) is 3.17x (>30%), favoring top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.867. direction_focus preset chosen due to avg POCID=38.1 < 45 (low directional accuracy).

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No ≥4 of same type exists. All candidates are safe from leakage (baselines never leak; selection/weighted use anti-leakage rolling/expanding per eval_config). Universe leaderboards show no candidate that dominates all current types. Score margin top2=0.014 indicates balanced set. No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=4.46 ≥ 0.25) favors trimmed_mean and robust methods. Stability issue (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.68, unstable=true) requires robust_median. n_windows=3 is low, so stronger regularization needed - adding trimmed_mean_r0.25 provides better RMSE (66.29 vs current ~70). Universe shows trimmed_mean_r0.30 has best RMSE at 66.17. Current set already has dba_combination and topk selection methods, no need to add more weighted candidates.
